title: progress tracking:

criteria.description: EditorObject interface in types.ts contains field `status?: 'planned' | 'in-progress' | 'completed'`
criteria.how_to_verify: Read client/src/lib/types.ts and confirm the status field exists on EditorObject with the exact union type.

criteria.description: PropertiesPanel renders a Select with options 'Planned', 'In Progress', 'Completed' when at least one object is selected
criteria.how_to_verify: Read PropertiesPanel.tsx and confirm a Select component exists whose onValueChange calls handleUpdate({ status: value }) with one of the three string literals.
criteria.evidence: File client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx, lines 122-140 implements a Select component with the required options and handleUpdate({ status: v as any }) call.

criteria.description: PropertiesPanel status Select shows empty/mixed placeholder when multiple selected objects have differing status values
criteria.how_to_verify: Read PropertiesPanel.tsx and confirm the Select value prop evaluates to '' or undefined when selectedObjects have non-uniform status values.
criteria.evidence: File client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx, line 124: value={selectedObjects.every(o => o.status === firstObject.status) ? (firstObject.status || '') : ''}. Placeholder is set to 'Mixed status' on line 128.

criteria.description: ObjectRenderer renders a status dot only when obj.status is defined; dot colour is gray for 'planned', amber for 'in-progress', green for 'completed'
criteria.how_to_verify: Read ObjectRenderer.tsx and confirm a conditional element exists that maps the three status values to Tailwind colour classes bg-gray-400 / bg-amber-400 / bg-green-500.
criteria.evidence: File client/src/components/editor/Canvas/ObjectRenderer.tsx, lines 167-176 implements the conditional rendering of the status dot using bg-gray-400, bg-amber-400, and bg-green-500 classes.

criteria.description: LayerPanel computes completedCount and totalCount from docState.objects filtering out type === 'path', and passes completedCount/totalCount * 100 to a Progress component
criteria.how_to_verify: Read LayerPanel.tsx and confirm the computation and the Progress component import/usage exist.
criteria.evidence: File client/src/components/editor/LayerPanel.tsx, lines 125-132 compute the counts excluding 'path' types, and line 155 passes progressPercent to the Progress component.

criteria.description: LayerPanel progress section is not rendered when docState.layers is empty (no project loaded)
criteria.how_to_verify: Read LayerPanel.tsx and confirm the progress section is inside a conditional that checks docState.layers.length > 0.
criteria.evidence: File client/src/components/editor/LayerPanel.tsx, line 143 wraps the progress section in {state.layers.length > 0 && (...)}.

criteria.description: TypeScript compiler finds no new errors in the four modified files (types.ts, PropertiesPanel.tsx, ObjectRenderer.tsx, LayerPanel.tsx)
criteria.how_to_verify: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` from the project root and confirm zero errors attributable to the changed files.
criteria.evidence: npx tsc --noEmit reports only 1 pre-existing error in Canvas.tsx:25, which is unrelated to the changes. No errors found in the modified files.

+
+### Gemini (Programista)
+
+**Open criteria do adresowania:**
+- `[c1]` W MobileBottomBar.tsx nie ma importu useObjectCreation ani żadnego wywołania handleAddIcon lub handleAddText
+- `[c2]` W MobileBottomBar.tsx nie ma sekcji JSX z nagłówkiem 'Add Object' ani przyciskami tworzącymi obiekty (circle, camera, square, triangle, hexagon, arrow-right, text)

+- `[c5]` W trybie 'list' MobileBottomBar nadal renderuje listę obiektów (Layer Objects) umożliwiającą ich zaznaczenie

+**Implementation Report:**
+
+# Implementation Report — Iteration 1
+
+## Changes made
+- Removed `useObjectCreation` hook import and usage from `MobileBottomBar.tsx`.
+- Removed unused icon imports from `lucide-react` (`Trash2`, `Circle`, `Square`, `Triangle`, `Hexagon`, `Camera`, `ArrowRight`).
+- Removed the "Add Object" section and its associated buttons from the mobile bottom bar in `list` mode.
+- Removed the "Delete Object" button and its preceding separator from the mobile bottom bar in `edit` mode.
+- Ensured that object name editing (`Input` with `UPDATE_OBJECTS`) and photo gallery (`ObjectPhotoGallery`) remain functional in `edit` mode.
+- Ensured that the object list ("Layer Objects") remains functional in `list` mode for selecting objects.
+
+## Files affected
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx`
+
+## Deviations from plan
+None
+
+## Potential issues
+None
+
+<details>
+<summary>Gemini stdout (raw)</summary>
+
+```
+I have restricted the mobile interface functionality in `MobileBottomBar.tsx` as requested. Users can now only edit object labels and manage photos on mobile devices; the options to add or delete objects have been removed.
+
+### Summary of Changes:
+- **Removed Object Creation:** The "Add Object" section, including all shape and text creation buttons, has been removed from the mobile view.
+- **Removed Object Deletion:** The "Delete Object" button has been removed from the object edit mode.
+- **Cleaned Up Imports:** Unused hook (`useObjectCreation`) and icon imports (`Circle`, `Square`, `Triangle`, `Hexagon`, `Camera`, `ArrowRight`, `Trash2`) have been removed.
+- **Maintained Core Mobile Features:** The ability to rename objects and use the `ObjectPhotoGallery` remains fully functional in the mobile editor mode. The object list ("Layer Objects") also remains available for selection.
+
+Verified all changes against the acceptance criteria using targeted searches and code review. Detailed records can be found in `implementation_report.md`.
+```
+</details>
+
+---
+
+## REVIEWING — iter 1 — 2026-05-01 14:39:22
+

+
+**Overall:** ✅ APPROVED
+
+**Criteria:**
+- ✅ `[c1]` **DONE** — File 'client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx' does not contain 'useObjectCreation', 'handleAddIcon', or 'handleAddText'. Verified by reading the entire file.

+- ✅ `[c4]` **DONE** — Lines 116-124 contain the Input for name editing with UPDATE_OBJECTS dispatch, and line 128 renders ObjectPhotoGallery.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c5]` **DONE** — Lines 75-104 render the 'Layer Objects' list, and line 91 handles the SELECT_OBJECT dispatch on click.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c6]` **DONE** — The lucide-react import block (lines 4-11) only contains ChevronUp, ChevronDown, ChevronLeft, Plus, and Type. The requested icons have been removed.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+
+**Blocking issues:**
+*None*

+**Suggestions:**

+**Next focus:** The implementation is correct and meets all acceptance criteria. No further action needed.

human_feedback: Follow-up request: przeanalizuj zadanie ./orchestrator/TASK-D66932/task.md i zaimplementuj w opraciu o te wytyczne
fix_plan:
Root cause: The previous implementation used a simplified, incorrect status set ('planned', 'in-progress', 'completed') and a UI (colored dots) that ignored the comprehensive requirements in TASK-D66932/task.md, including metadata tracking and direct icon colorization.

Fix steps:
  1. Update EditorObject type in client/src/lib/types.ts to include the correct status union (PLANNED, CABLE_PULLED, TERMINATED, TESTED, APPROVED, ISSUE) and add fields statusUpdatedAt, statusUpdatedBy, and issueDescription. (files: client/src/lib/types.ts)
  2. Implement a UserIdentificationModal that stores the technician's name in localStorage and ensure it is displayed on app launch (App.tsx) if no name is found. (files: client/src/App.tsx)
  3. Replace the Select in PropertiesPanel.tsx with a grid of status buttons, ensuring each click also sets statusUpdatedAt and statusUpdatedBy, and conditionally show a text field for issueDescription. (files: client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx)
  4. Modify ObjectRenderer.tsx to map statuses to specific colors (e.g., TESTED -> Jasnozielony) and apply these colors directly to the icon/text component, removing the absolute-positioned status dot. (files: client/src/components/editor/Canvas/ObjectRenderer.tsx)
  5. Update useObjectCreation.ts to set the default status of all newly created objects to 'PLANNED'. (files: client/src/hooks/useObjectCreation.ts)
  6. Refactor the summary section in LayerPanel.tsx to display statistics based on the new status model (Total, Completed: TESTED/APPROVED, and Issues). (files: client/src/components/editor/LayerPanel.tsx)

Key fix: Re-implement the progress tracking feature to strictly match the TASK-D66932 requirements, prioritizing the 6-status model, metadata persistence, and direct icon colorization.
